stories.title: New-build homes should not house migrants, says Home Office
stories.summary: The Home Office stated that new-build homes 'should never house asylum seekers' after reports emerged that up to 83 migrants would be placed at a new development.

stories.title: More than 300 arrests in organised immigration crime crackdown
stories.summary: A total of 362 arrests were made as part of a major crackdown on organised immigration crime, following the launch of a new £30m High Street organised crime unit announced in May.

stories.title: Venezuela earthquakes: what you can do to help
stories.summary: The FCDO published guidance on how members of the public can support people affected by the earthquakes in Venezuela.

stories.title: Nigeria travel advice updated: FCDO advises against travel to Kwara and Benue States
stories.summary: The FCDO updated its Nigeria travel advice, now advising against all but essential travel to Kwara State and Benue State due to security concerns.

stories.title: English-language training programme – Central Asia: invitation to tender
stories.summary: The FCDO invited proposals by 17 July 2026 to provide an English-language training programme across Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan.

stories.title: Brighton's first Youth Guarantee Jobs Fair sees hundreds of young people connect with local employers
stories.summary: Over 650 young people attended Brighton's first ever Youth Guarantee Jobs Fair at the Brighton Centre, part of the government's nationwide Youth Guarantee initiative.

agencies.name: DEFRA
agencies.description: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs

stories.title: How SFI is helping a small farm improve profitability and resilience
stories.summary: DEFRA's Farming Blog shared a case study on how the Sustainable Farming Incentive (SFI) scheme is helping smaller farms improve their financial resilience and sustainability.

stories.title: PM2.5 air quality targets updated
stories.summary: DEFRA published updated official statistics on PM2.5 air quality targets (PERT and AMCT), tracking progress against environmental targets.

stories.title: South West of England highest risk for tick bites and Lyme disease
stories.summary: A BBC report citing DEFRA data found the South West has the highest rate of laboratory-confirmed Lyme disease cases in England, with 281 recorded in 2025.

stories.title: HSE authorises Crown Censure of MoD over fatal Challenger 2 tank explosion
stories.summary: The Health and Safety Executive authorised a Crown Censure of the Ministry of Defence and a prosecution of a defence contractor following the deaths of Corporal Darren Neilson and Corporal Matthew Hatfield when an L30 gun exploded on a Challenger 2 tank at Castlemartin Range in Pembrokeshire.

stories.title: Badenoch accuses Starmer of leaving defence spending gap
stories.summary: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch criticised the government over what she called a missing £5bn in the defence investment plan.

stories.title: Windsor horse show death investigation could take months
stories.summary: An investigation into the death of Lance Bombardier Ciara Sullivan, 24, who fell from her horse at the 15 May Windsor Castle show, is expected to take several months to conclude.

stories.title: UK's new steel trade measure takes effect from 1 July 2026
stories.summary: From 1 July 2026, the government began limiting tariff-free steel imports, reducing overall quota volumes by 51% compared to the previous steel safeguard measure, following an announcement in March 2026.

stories.title: Critical Minerals Programme: Magnet Hub — up to £20m available
stories.summary: The Department for Business and Trade announced it will provide up to £20 million to fund a dedicated Magnet Hub as part of the Critical Minerals Programme, aimed at strengthening UK supply chain resilience.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: Women in Research Charter published
stories.summary: DSIT published the Women in Research Charter, complementing the Women in Tech Taskforce's work to ensure more talented women can enter, stay in, and lead the UK's science and technology sectors.

stories.title: Engineering biology for critical minerals — policy briefing
stories.summary: DSIT published a policy briefing on using biology-based approaches to recover critical minerals from secondary resources, highlighting opportunities to improve UK supply chain resilience.

stories.title: Thousands more vulnerable adults to be offered RSV vaccine
stories.summary: NHS England announced that thousands more vulnerable adults will be offered the RSV vaccine to prevent serious lung infections, as part of expanded winter vaccination planning from 1 July 2026.

stories.title: NHS to take over troubled mental health hospital
stories.summary: An NHS trust will take over the care of hundreds of patients from a private mental health provider that is under investigation by police and the Charity Commission.

stories.title: Urgent Question in Parliament over Royal Mail's failure to meet universal service obligation
stories.summary: MPs raised an urgent question in the House of Commons regarding Royal Mail's failure to meet its universal service obligation, reflecting ongoing concerns about delivery standards across the UK.
